generated_comment: These days people only blame the husband, but if a woman knows very well that a man is married and still keeps getting close, what do you call that? Bilkul serial wali situation — such women knowingly disturb families, and the man may be foolish, but she is the one allowing it and pulling him in. Someone has to think about the wife and children before becoming so friendly.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places primary blame on the woman in an affair scenario while minimizing the man's responsibility. It encodes homewrecker framing, 'she knew he was married' logic, and male victimhood/seduction framing.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 41. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are posting under your real name on a parenting Facebook group after a thread was started by a childless woman by choice who wrote about feeling judged at family gatherings and asked for advice on how to handle relatives who keep asking when she will have children. You are writing a comment responding to her post and giving your opinion on her situation and whether the relatives asking her are actually wrong and what you think her life is missing that she cannot see yet.
